title: Folding Guitar Stand
summary: A curved, single-guitar stand that pivots on a dowel rod and folds flat for travel or storage.
description:
A fixed guitar stand takes up floor space whether or not it's holding a guitar; this one pivots flat on a dowel-rod hinge so it folds down to almost nothing when it's not in use — worth the extra build step if the stand is going in a gig bag or a small apartment.

The curved silhouette isn't just decorative — the profile is what the guitar's body and neck actually rest against, so trace it carefully rather than freehanding a rough approximation.

A small latch piece, pivoting on its own screw, is what holds the stand open under the guitar's weight; without it the two halves would simply fold closed again.

tools.note: Or a band saw for the curved profile.
Trues up the glued-up blank's edge.

cutList.note: Trace and cut the curved profile from this blank.

steps.title: True up the blank
steps.body: If needed, join boards edge-to-edge to reach a 9-1/2in wide blank, then true up one edge on a table saw.

steps.title: Cut the curved profile
steps.body: Trace and cut the curved stand profile onto each blank with a jigsaw.

steps.title: Drill the pivot hole
steps.body: Drill a hole sized to the 3/4in dowel rod through both stand halves at the fold point, so they can pivot on the dowel.

steps.title: Build and attach the latch
steps.body: Cut and drill the small latch piece that holds the stand open, and attach it to one half with a screw so it pivots freely.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand progressively from 150 to 220 grit, apply felt strip tape to every point that will contact the guitar, and finish.
